headline: How Investors May Respond To Southern (SO) Extending Its 25-Year Dividend Growth Streak
summary: Earlier in April, Southern Company announced it is raising its regular quarterly dividend to US$0.76 per share, payable June 8, 2026, lifting its annualized payout to US$3.04. This increase marks the utility’s 25th consecutive year of higher dividends, underscoring a long-running emphasis on consistent cash returns to shareholders. We’ll now examine how this 25-year dividend growth streak shapes Southern’s investment narrative and what it may mean for income-focused investors. Rare earth...
